items.q: What is the significance of Duchamp's 'Fountain'?
items.correct: It challenged the definition of art - the artist's choice, not their skill, becomes the artwork.
items.random_wrong: It is a study of plumbing.
items.why: Students miss the conceptual point. Duchamp didn't make the urinal - he selected and presented it. The radical claim is that ART IS DESIGNATION, opening the door to all conceptual art.

items.q: What did Pollock mean by his drip paintings being 'in the painting'?
items.correct: He worked from all sides, making the act of painting central to the meaning.
items.misconception: He literally fell into wet paint.
items.why: Students take 'in the painting' literally. Pollock meant his BODILY ENGAGEMENT - working around and over the canvas on the floor - was integral to the work, not just the marks left behind.

items.q: What attitude does Pop Art take toward consumer culture?
items.correct: Ambiguous - it both celebrates and critiques mass media imagery.
items.why: Students read Pop Art as either pure celebration or pure protest. Warhol's deadpan repetition of Marilyn or soup cans is deliberately AMBIGUOUS, leaving viewers to decide if it's homage or critique.

items.q: What does "coherence" mean in a project?
items.correct: The project tells one clear story
items.why: It's The project tells one clear story. “The work is glossy” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Documentation should ideally be lit with:
items.why: It's Even, neutral light. “Strong single spotlight” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Minimum number of angles to photograph a 3D outcome from?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
